message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer B) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-8, latest commit). Your job: confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; the regression test actually DISCRIMINATES — fails if the fix is reverted). Be skeptical; try to find a hole.

FINDING K-CI-2:
CI ran the bare `pytest` console script which does not put the repo root on sys.path, so `from tests import ...` failed at collection (ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests') — the suite never ran in CI. Fix: pyproject [tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath now ["src", "."] so the `tests` package imports under bare `pytest` too. Verify: `cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && pytest -q --co 2>&1 | tail -2` collects (was ModuleNotFoundError). Test: test_pytest_path_includes_repo_root_so_tests_package_imports.

Read the actual changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path> — use "python -m pytest", not bare pytest, for your own runs; uv 0.11.21 is available for K-OPS-1). Judge ONLY K-CI-2. Return CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name it); REJECTED if wrong or non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding K-CI-2. Let me examine the changed files and run verification.

d08f031 fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
d2ee0f4 docs(hardening): correct pass-7 PR ref to #168; open pass 8
57d9355 Merge pull request #168 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-7

---DIFF---
commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

82:[tool.pytest.ini_options]
83-testpaths = ["tests"]
84-# "src" puts the package on the path; "." (the rootdir) puts the repo root on sys.path so the
85-# `tests` package itself is importable (several suites do `from tests import reference_study` and
86-# `from tests.integration.conftest import ...`). Without it, the bare `pytest` console script CI
87-# runs — unlike `python -m pytest`, which prepends CWD — fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No
88-# module named 'tests'`, so the whole CI test step errored at collection (#K-CI-2).
89-pythonpath = ["src", "."]
90-filterwarnings = [
91-    # The unkeyed-trail advisory (#F-049) is intentional and fires whenever run_study is called
92-    # without a secret — most tests deliberately use the unkeyed reproducible path. Suppress the
93-    # noise here; test_run_study_warns_when_trail_is_unkeyed asserts the warning fires explicitly.
94-    "ignore:run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED:UserWarning",
95-    # The no-recorder gate-node advisory (#F-007) likewise fires in many graph tests that drive a
96-    # gate node without a provenance recorder; test_make_gate_node_warns_when_no_recorder asserts
97-    # it explicitly.

content.text: Let me look at the regression test and the CI workflow change.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git show HEAD -- tests/test_supply_chain.py | grep -n -A40 "pytest_path_includes_repo_root"

content.content:
54:+def test_pytest_path_includes_repo_root_so_tests_package_imports():
55-+    # #K-CI-2: CI runs the bare `pytest` console script which (unlike `python -m pytest`) does NOT
56-+    # prepend CWD to sys.path, so `from tests import ...` failed at collection with
57-+    # ModuleNotFoundError and the whole CI test step errored. The pytest pythonpath must include
58-+    # the repo root (".") so the `tests` package imports regardless of invocation.
59-+    import tomllib
60-+
61-+    pp = tomllib.loads((_ROOT / "pyproject.toml").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
62-+    paths = pp["tool"]["pytest"]["ini_options"]["pythonpath"]
63-+    assert "." in paths, "repo root not on pytest pythonpath — bare `pytest` cannot import `tests`"
64-+
65-+
66-+def test_mypy_ignores_httpx_missing_stub_in_core_lane():
67-+    # #K-CI-1: seam.py imports httpx lazily (guarded) for the BULK-tier HTTP timeout; httpx ships
68-+    # only with the reasoning extra, so the dependency-free core lane has no stub and mypy --strict
69-+    # failed there. httpx must be in the ignore-missing-imports override list like the other
70-+    # optional backends.
71-+    import tomllib
72-+
73-+    pp = tomllib.loads((_ROOT / "pyproject.toml").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
74-+    overrides = pp["tool"]["mypy"]["overrides"]
75-+    modules = {m for o in overrides if o.get("ignore_missing_imports") for m in o.get("module", [])}
76-+    assert "httpx.*" in modules, "httpx not in mypy ignore_missing_imports — core lane breaks"
77-+
78-+
79-+def test_coverage_floor_is_enforced():
80-+    # #K-OPS-3: `pytest --cov` without a threshold exits 0 at any coverage, so the gate was vacuous.
81-+    text = _ci_text()
82-+    assert "--cov-fail-under=" in text, "coverage measured but not enforced (vacuous gate)"
83-+
84-+
85-+def test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned():
86-+    # #K-OPS-1: the lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI, so it went red
87-+    # whenever any in-range transitive release landed (it was failing on a clean checkout). The
88-+    # resolution must be pinned to a fixed point-in-time index via --exclude-newer, sourced from a
89-+    # single committed date file so CI and the regen script agree.
90-+    text = _ci_text()
91-+    assert "--exclude-newer" in text, "lockfile sync re-resolves against live PyPI (#K-OPS-1)"
92-+    assert (_ROOT / ".uv-exclude-newer").exists(), "missing .uv-exclude-newer date source"
93-+    date = (_ROOT / ".uv-exclude-newer").read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
94-+    assert date and date[0].isdigit(), "exclude-newer date is empty/malformed"

11:    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
12:      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
54:-        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
55:+        # --cov-fail-under ENFORCES a floor (#K-OPS-3): without it `pytest --cov` prints coverage
59:+        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=90

content.text: The CI still uses bare `pytest`, which is exactly the failing scenario. The fix correctly addresses the root cause (pythonpath += ".") rather than changing CI to `python -m pytest`. Now let me verify the test discriminates and that collection works under bare pytest.

input.description: Collect via bare pytest console script

content.text: Bare `pytest` now collects 560 tests (clean). Now the discrimination test: revert the fix (remove "." from pythonpath) and confirm both that bare pytest collection breaks AND that the regression test fails.

content.text: Both confirmed: with the fix reverted, bare pytest errors at collection (ModuleNotFoundError on `from tests import ...`), and the regression test fails. Let me confirm the error is exactly the ModuleNotFoundError claimed, then restore.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && pytest -q --co tests/test_pipeline.py 2>&1 | grep -i "modulenot\|No module" | head -3; cp /tmp/pyproject.bak pyproject.toml && echo "restored"; grep -n 'pythonpath' pyproject.toml
input.description: Confirm error type and restore file

content.content:
E   ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'
restored
89:pythonpath = ["src", "."]

content.text:
Confirmed and restored. The error is exactly `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'` as claimed.

Assessment:

**Appropriate** — Root cause correctly identified: the bare `pytest` console script (still used in CI per ci.yml line 59) does not prepend CWD to sys.path, unlike `python -m pytest`. The fix is at the right layer: it makes the suite collect under *any* invocation via `pythonpath = ["src", "."]` in pyproject, rather than the brittle alternative of changing CI to `python -m pytest` (which would leave the latent fragility). The `tests` package is importable from the repo root, so adding "." is the correct resolution.

**Complete** — The regression test discriminates correctly: with "." removed, the test fails (`assert '.' in ['src']`) and bare pytest errors at collection. With the fix in place, bare pytest collects 560 tests cleanly and the test passes.

Minor note (not a gap): the test asserts the config invariant rather than driving an actual subprocess `pytest` collection. That is an acceptable proxy — it pins the exact knob and would catch a revert — but it would not catch a hypothetical future regression where collection breaks for a different reason (e.g., conftest path manipulation removed) while "." remains present. The direct empirical check I ran confirms real collection works, so this is a robustness observation, not a residual defect for K-CI-2.

input.rationale: Root cause correctly identified and fixed at the right layer. CI still invokes the bare `pytest` console script (ci.yml line 59: `pytest -q --cov=...`), which—unlike `python -m pytest`—does not prepend CWD to sys.path; suites doing `from tests import ...` failed at collection. The fix sets pythonpath = ["src", "."] in pyproject [tool.pytest.ini_options], making the `tests` package importable under ANY invocation. This is the correct, durable approach (vs. the brittle alternative of changing CI to `python -m pytest`). Verification: with the fix, bare `pytest -q --co` collects 560 tests cleanly and the regression test passes. Discrimination proven: reverting to pythonpath=["src"] makes bare pytest fail collection with exactly `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'` (5 collection errors) AND the regression test fails (`assert '.' in ['src']`). Fix is both appropriate and complete.
concerns: Non-blocking robustness note: the regression test asserts the pyproject config invariant ("." present in pythonpath) rather than driving an actual subprocess bare-`pytest` collection. It pins the exact knob and catches a revert of THIS fix, but would not catch a future regression where collection breaks for a different reason (e.g., conftest sys.path manipulation removed) while "." remains. Direct empirical check confirms real collection works, so this is not a residual defect for K-CI-2.

content.text: Verdict: CONFIRMED. K-CI-2 fix is both appropriate (correct root cause, right layer) and complete (regression test discriminates — reverting reproduces the exact `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests'` and fails the test).
